name: John White
skill: Taking history, vital signs, and giving directions
scenario: Broken arm after an accident three hours ago.
introAudioText: Patient 1. John White. Broken arm after an accident three hours ago.
lines: Good morning. My name is _____. I am a practical nurse.
Good morning. My name is blank. I am a practical nurse.
May I ask your name, please?
May I ask your name, please?
Yes, I am allergic to paracetamol.
Yes, I am allergic to paracetamol.
May I check your vital signs now?
May I check your vital signs now?
Your Blood pressure is 126/82 millimeters of mercury.
Your blood pressure is one hundred twenty-six over eighty-two millimeters of mercury.
Your Respiratory rate is 18 breaths per minute.
Your respiratory rate is eighteen breaths per minute.
Your Pulse rate is 86 beats per minute.
Your pulse rate is eighty-six beats per minute.
The X-ray room is on the second floor. Please wait for the doctor.
The X-ray room is on the second floor. Please wait for the doctor.
You are welcome. Have a good day.
You are welcome. Have a good day.

name: Thomas Blake
scenario: Chest pain for two days with history of heart disease.
introAudioText: Patient 2. Thomas Blake. Chest pain for two days. History of heart disease.
lines: Good morning. My name is _____. I am a practical nurse.
Good morning. My name is blank. I am a practical nurse.
May I ask your name, please?
May I ask your name, please?
Do you have any underlying diseases?
Do you have any underlying diseases?
This is important. I will call the doctor and check your vital signs now.
This is important. I will call the doctor and check your vital signs now.
Your Blood pressure is 146/92 millimeters of mercury.
Your blood pressure is one hundred forty-six over ninety-two millimeters of mercury.
Your Pulse rate is 104 beats per minute.
Your pulse rate is one hundred four beats per minute.
The cardiology clinic is on the second floor. Please stay seated and try to stay calm.
The cardiology clinic is on the second floor. Please stay seated and try to stay calm.

name: Peter Brown
scenario: Diarrhea for three days with no allergies and no underlying disease.
introAudioText: Patient 3. Peter Brown. Diarrhea for three days. No allergies and no underlying disease.
lines: Good morning. My name is _____. I am a practical nurse.
Good morning. My name is blank. I am a practical nurse.
How long have you had diarrhea?
How long have you had diarrhea?
Do you have vomiting or abdominal pain?
Do you have vomiting or abdominal pain?
Yes, I have abdominal pain, but no vomiting.
Yes, I have abdominal pain, but no vomiting.
We need a stool test and a urine test to check the cause.
We need a stool test and a urine test to check the cause.
Please drink small sips of water and wait for the doctor.
Please drink small sips of water and wait for the doctor.
